strategy: Inspect the 01:20-01:25 screen-recording interval with a small chronological frame set, OCR any visible price-bearing items, then extract all item-price pairs and compute the total against the options.

steps.purpose: Retrieve a chronological set of frames spanning 01:20-01:25 so the agent can verify whether any price-bearing items are still visible in that exact interval and preserve any changing screen state.

inputs.query: frames in 01:20-01:25 showing any visible on-screen items with price points, if present

expected_outputs.frames: chronological frames from the exact interval for visual verification of visible priced items

steps.purpose: Read all visible on-screen text in those frames, especially dollar amounts and nearby item names or labels.

inputs.query: read all visible item names, labels, and dollar prices on the screen; preserve exact dollar amounts and nearby product text

expected_outputs.text: raw OCR text from the interval
expected_outputs.lines: OCR lines containing item labels and price amounts

steps.purpose: From the frames and OCR, determine every distinct visible item that has a visible price point during 01:20-01:25, deduplicate repeated appearances across frames, sum the prices, and map to the answer option.

inputs.query: Using the exact 01:20-01:25 frames and OCR, identify every distinct visible item that has a visible price point during this interval. For each item, extract the item name and exact price, deduplicate the same item across multiple frames, exclude anything without a visible price in this interval, compute the total cost, and state which option matches.

expected_outputs.supporting_points: list of item-price pairs included and any exclusions

refinement_instructions: Do not trust preprocess summaries as proof of prices. Base the final reasoning on the retrieved 01:20-01:25 frames plus OCR. If no priced items are actually visible in that exact interval, say so rather than borrowing prices from 01:08-01:13. If multiple frames show the same product page, count each distinct item once. Preserve exact item-price pairings and reject OCR fragments that are not clearly attached to an item or are not dollar prices.
